score_injection_action: Rank=Disgraced/<0 with close_fail_qa_pass(-12) and user_rating_1(-20) recent signals: applied maximum adversarial rigor — ran actual jq commands against the target file to prove error mode (not assumed), simulated fixed-file to confirm the corrected path works, verified ticket and context JSON prose for the same broken .[] path duplication Codex flagged, and confirmed OBJ-1/OBJ-2/OBJ-4 are fully resolved before naming one remaining blocker.

qa.overall_assessment: OBJ-1 (F6/F7 Must Have), OBJ-2 (AC-F5 section-scoped), and OBJ-4 (AC-F1 negative patterns) are fully resolved. OBJ-3 is still blocking: BA replaced the false-pass grep with jq commands, but the jq_filter uses '.[]' which errors (exit 5, not true/false) on acceptance-criteria-20260527-132200.json because that file is a dict wrapper '{"acceptance_criteria":[...]}' not a bare array. The jq path must be '.acceptance_criteria[]'. This error also appears verbatim in the ticket prose (lines 255-259) and context JSON (codex_consult.findings), making the BA spec internally inconsistent about the verification recipe. One additional major finding from Codex: AC-F5 proves sentinel variable exists and an if-pattern exists, but does not prove the Agent dispatch block is inside the conditional — a static section structure check would be needed for full verification assurance.

objection_resolution_summary.description: F6 and F7 promoted to Must Have in ticket and context JSON
objection_resolution_summary.evidence: ticket-20260529-080709.md lines 133-134 list F6 and F7 under Must Have; Should Have section reads '(all items promoted to Must Have — none remain)'; Goal section lines 14-16 state 'Codex findings table...explicitly marks AC12 test (F6) and AC6 spec drift (F7) as Fix required — these are Must Have, not optional'; context JSON clarified field says '5 blocking + 2 fix-required'; context JSON scope.included entries 6 and 7 list F6 and F7

objection_resolution_summary.description: AC-F5 uses section-anchored patterns referencing GRAPHIFY_SENTINEL.*graphify.json within graphify dispatch block
objection_resolution_summary.evidence: acceptance-criteria-20260529-080709.json AC-F5 check.patterns use section_anchor='Graphify enrichment' and section_end='### Step 8' for both pattern entries. Pattern 1: 'GRAPHIFY_SENTINEL=.*graphify\.json'. Pattern 2: 'if.*-f.*GRAPHIFY_SENTINEL\|if.*GRAPHIFY_SENTINEL.*exists'. The offending line 712 text ('dispatch MUST be skipped on this cycle') is under '### Step 8' heading — confirmed outside the scoped section by reading commands/dev.md:710-715. The old false-pass 'skip.*dispatch' pattern is absent.

objection_resolution_summary.description: AC-F7 uses jq-based check that fails on the unfixed acceptance-criteria file (not a grep that pre-matches)
objection_resolution_summary.evidence: BA replaced the grep with jq commands. However: (1) acceptance-criteria-20260527-132200.json has structure {"task_id":..., "acceptance_criteria":[...]} — a dict wrapper, not a bare array. (2) The AC-F7 jq_filter uses '.[] | select(.id=="AC6")' which iterates top-level dict keys (task_id, generated_at, acceptance_criteria) and errors with 'Cannot index string with string "id"' (exit 5) on the string values. Tested: jq exit 5 on both UNFIXED and FIXED simulated file. (3) The correct path is '.acceptance_criteria[] | select(.id=="AC6")'. (4) Using the correct path: UNFIXED file returns false (cmd1) and exit 0 with 'true' (cmd2 — old pattern still present) — correctly discriminates. (5) Same broken '.[]' path appears in ticket-20260529-080709.md lines 255-259 and in context-20260529-080709.json codex_consult.findings (proposed_fix field still references old '.[]' form). The check cannot verify criterion was met on either the unfixed or fixed file.
objection_resolution_summary.required_fix: In acceptance-criteria-20260529-080709.json, ticket-20260529-080709.md (lines 255-259), and context-20260529-080709.json codex_consult.findings[OBJ-3].proposed_fix: replace '.[] | select(.id=="AC6")' with '.acceptance_criteria[] | select(.id=="AC6")' in all AC-F7 jq_filter values. Alternatively use 'any(.acceptance_criteria[]; .id=="AC6" and (.when | test("prose"; "i")))' idiom which avoids the select-on-wrapper issue entirely.

objection_resolution_summary.description: AC-F1 includes negative patterns asserting bare python3 graphify calls absent
objection_resolution_summary.evidence: acceptance-criteria-20260529-080709.json AC-F1 check.patterns has 4 no_match entries: (1) agents/graphify.md '^[[:space:]]*python3[[:space:]].*graphify-enrich' expect no_match; (2) commands/dev.md '^[[:space:]]*python3[[:space:]].*graphify-query' expect no_match; (3) commands/dev.md '^[[:space:]]*Run:[[:space:]]*python3[[:space:]].*graphify-enrich' expect no_match; (4) commands/pull.md '^[[:space:]]*python3[[:space:]].*graphify-maintain' expect no_match. These cover the specific 'Run:' prefix variant in commands/dev.md per Codex OBJ-4 finding.

ba_validation_dimensions.details: BA's file-level evidence accuracy is unchanged from pass 1 (all verified). OBJ-1 fix added correct goal language citing close report explicitly. OBJ-2 fix added verifiable section_anchor/section_end fields. OBJ-4 fix added correctly-anchored negative patterns. All evidence claims continue to hold.

OBJ-1 resolved: F6 and F7 are now Must Have in ticket MoSCoW, ticket Goal, context JSON clarified field, and context JSON scope.included. The universalist '全部' requirement is fully satisfied by the Must Have list covering all 7 items. Should Have section explicitly states all items promoted.
All prior BA investigation evidence carried forward correctly. No new investigation gaps introduced.
File paths all verified. The addition of docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260527-132200.json to F7 files_to_modify is correct and previously confirmed by Read.

OBJ-3 remains unresolved. BA replaced the false-pass grep with jq commands, but the jq_filter '.[] | select(.id=="AC6")' is broken for the dict-wrapper file structure of acceptance-criteria-20260527-132200.json. The commands error (exit 5) on both the unfixed and the fixed file — they cannot distinguish fixed from unfixed state. The correct filter is '.acceptance_criteria[] | select(.id=="AC6")'. Additionally, Codex identified that the same broken '.[]' path is duplicated verbatim in ticket-20260529-080709.md lines 255-259 and context-20260529-080709.json codex_consult.findings, leaving the BA spec internally inconsistent. The section_grep (3rd command, for the markdown ticket) is correctly structured and would work, but it alone cannot substitute for the JSON verification of the AC6 entry in acceptance-criteria-20260527-132200.json.

blocking_finding.proof: jq -e '.[] | select(.id=="AC6") | .when | test("prose";"i")' docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260527-132200.json -> exit 5, error 'Cannot index string with string id'. Simulated fixed file with correct path: '.acceptance_criteria[] | select(.id=="AC6")' returns 'false' (cmd1, unfixed) and 'true' (cmd1, fixed), correctly discriminating. '.[]' path returns exit 5 on both.
blocking_finding.duplication_in_prose: ticket-20260529-080709.md lines 255-259 and context-20260529-080709.json codex_consult.findings[OBJ-3].proposed_fix both prescribe the wrong '.[]' path

failures.issue: Both jq_filter values use '.[] | select(.id=="AC6")' but the target file acceptance-criteria-20260527-132200.json is a dict wrapper with key 'acceptance_criteria' — '.[]' errors (exit 5) on string top-level values rather than returning true/false. The check cannot verify the AC6 fix was applied. The same broken path also appears in ticket-20260529-080709.md lines 255-259 and context-20260529-080709.json codex_consult findings, leaving the BA spec inconsistent with itself.
failures.recommendation: Replace '.[] | select(.id=="AC6")' with '.acceptance_criteria[] | select(.id=="AC6")' in all three locations: (1) acceptance-criteria-20260529-080709.json AC-F7 jq_filter lines; (2) ticket-20260529-080709.md VERIFICATION block (lines 255-259); (3) context-20260529-080709.json OBJ-3 proposed_fix text. Verify: jq -e '.acceptance_criteria[] | select(.id=="AC6") | .when | test("prose";"i")' on the unfixed file returns 'false' (exit 1) — correctly fails; on the fixed file returns 'true' (exit 0) — correctly passes.

codex_finding_4_assessment.codex_claim: AC-F5 proves sentinel variable exists and an if-pattern exists, but does not prove the Agent dispatch block is inside/guarded by the conditional.
codex_finding_4_assessment.rationale: Codex classified this as MAJOR (not BLOCKING). The section-scoped check is sufficient evidence that the sentinel variable and if-guard are in the correct section. A grep-based check cannot prove code flow by construction — this is a known limitation of pattern-based AC verification for control-flow assertions. The AC text correctly describes the expected behavior and the section-scoped grep provides meaningful signal that the required constructs are present in the right location. Upgrading this to a blocking objection would require a parser-based analysis beyond what grep/jq support. Logged as advisory for BA awareness.
codex_finding_4_assessment.recommendation: OBSERVATION_ONLY: BA may wish to add a structural note to AC-F5 acknowledging that section-scoped grep cannot prove control flow — this is an inherent grep limitation. No spec change required to unblock this cycle.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: AC-F5 section-scoped grep proves sentinel variable assignment and if-pattern presence but cannot statically prove the Agent dispatch code block is inside the if-conditional (Codex finding 4). This is a grep limitation inherent to pattern-based AC verification, not a spec defect. Advisory note for BA.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed OBJ-3 is blocking (finding 1): '.[] | select(.id=="AC6")' errors on the dict wrapper — proven via actual jq execution. Codex also identified that the same broken path is duplicated in ticket prose and context JSON (finding 2), and that section_grep alone cannot rescue the JSON verification (finding 3). Finding 4 (MAJOR): AC-F5 section-scoped grep proves variable presence but not that Agent dispatch is inside the if-block — classified as advisory rather than blocking per QA judgment. Finding 5: OBJ-1 and OBJ-4 confirmed resolved.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1, 2, 3 incorporated as one blocking failure (the jq path issue with its three-location manifestation). Finding 4 logged as advisory/out_of_scope_observation. Finding 5 confirms OBJ-1/OBJ-4 pass verdicts.
